title: Positive update on my last post!
author: No_Page5992
selftext:
Hey everyone, it's me again.

I wanted to give a quick update on our last testing phase on the bot I mentioned here a month ago, since quite a few people were interested back then.

We've now been running the test phase for some time, and honestly, it's been very interesting to see how things actually play out in practice.

The biggest thing we've learned is that the original idea still holds up extremely well.  
Which is creating a hybrid automated bot.  
Meaning, the user still needs to do their analysis.  
Combining human discretion with automated execution.  
  
Your analysis could be extremely simple, or extremely accurate.  
The bot has many different ways of use. Being very versatile which means the bot can be used by everyone.  
Whether you'd be a beginner, or a experienced trader.

What surprised me the most wasn't even my own results, but how others interacted with it.  
Which out of feedback, was extremely easy to use.

I mentioned that I'd test this bot initially with 3 others I believe..  
This testing group has expanded to 9 in total.  
Including several people with zero knowledge about the markets.

Seeing how differently people approach the same tool as actually helped us improve it a lot.

We've made several adjustments this month to make it easier to use, more accurate, and safer.  
Especially for people who don't have a strong trading background.

The goal is not to make it ''automatic'' but to make it structured and simple enough that it reduces most common mistakes, while only trading the highest probability trades.

Yes, this product will be for sale.  
Why?  
Because it is not fully automated.  
My vision, and my goal, is to create a community within this bot.  
Having people who actively contribute to making this bot better for the sake of all of us.  
As this is a very versatile hybrid bot, improvements will happen all the time.  
Me, my team, and all the clients will use this bot.   
What I'd love to see, if me and my team could actually learn from our clients, and improve on their demands.

Mid this month we were able to actually announce, that the bot works exactly like it's supposed to, which is why we've opened pre-orders.  
Pre-ordered clients will receive a invitation in a exclusive server in which me and the testers are in.  
They can follow all that is happening, and refund anytime if they do not like what they see, while still having the lowest possible price.

Our pricing will increase with demand, in phases.

Why?  
Because we aim to give the best support possible, all the time, to all our clients.  
This includes being in a exclusive server in which the client could ask any question,  
With purchase having the ability to plan a 1-to-1 onboarding call with me, in which I'll explain all you need, giving update reports, manuals, guides, all these kind of things.  
  
If for example 5000 people would buy this product, we will not be able to attend to every single client.  
Support is my personal main focus during this journey, since the trading industry lacks in this in my personal opinion.

To be clear, this is not meant to be a sales post, rather simply sharing my story.  
Why I share my story is simple. To share my vision, my goals, and what I do.  
I am merely interested in seeing actual responses, other points of views and what people's thoughts are on MY journey.

I simply want to connect, possibly meet new people to work with. Might find some new clients who could need a product like this, or simply show people that something like this can actually be real, and not some BS we'd find mostly in this industry.

If people are interested to see what this is about, feel free to shoot me a message.

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body:
**TL;DR:**
OP says they tested a “hybrid” trading bot (you do the analysis, bot handles structure/execution) with ~9 testers including beginners. They claim it’s easier to use, safer, and working as intended after a month. Pre-orders are open with access to a private server, onboarding calls, and ongoing updates. Positioning it as community-driven rather than fully automated.

**What’s still missing (important for evaluation):**

* no performance stats (returns, drawdown, trade count)
* no timeframe/instrument details
* unclear whether results are live or paper
* “highest-probability trades” without metrics = marketing language

Would be helpful to see an equity curve + max drawdown + sample size before people treat this as validated.

replies.author: No_Page5992
replies.body:
Hey!

First of all, thank you for the summary.  
Yes, of course proof of concept is necessary before people treat this as validated.  
This is why I am open for calls, simply to show how our hybrid bot works.

There is not a single timeframe to be used.  
As I mentioned, the bot is extremely versatile and can be optimised for any preference.  
For example:  
Are you a experienced trader?   
You could turn off several parameters, let the bot run on the 1 minute timeframe on for example supply and demand zones to have maximum potential.  
BUT, as we all understand, more potential comes with higher risk.  
For your knowledge, in the most agressive way of the bot, I've made 70% in a week.  
But such results only come with significant risk.  
A week later, it dropped with 30%, and this week up 20% again.  
This bot is meant to be used as a tool, making trading easier.

About your last POI.  
What I mean with the highest-probability trades, is what the bot looks for, in how it is designed to trade.  
The user waits for certain area's to turn the bot on, and after the bot has been turned on, it'll wait for several more confirmations.  
Creating a safety net on top of your ''entry / activation'' zone = highest probability trades

Lastly, to inform people and to show how I use the bot, and how it has performed on my end, on funded accounts!

This month:  
\- 20 trades  
\- 7 losses  
max consecutive losses = 3  
max consecutive wins = 6  
1 RR  
end result = +6%
